title: Enumeration types should follow a naming convention
description: Enumeration types should comply with a naming conventionAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?Shared naming conventions allow teams to collaborate efficiently. This rule checks that allenumnames match a provided regular expression.The default configuration is the one recommended by Microsoft:Pascal casing, starting with an upper case character, e.g. BackColorShort abbreviations of 2 letters can be capitalized, e.g.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Enum name not following PascalCase naming convention
logic.triggering_conditions: Enum declaration with name that doesn't match PascalCase pattern (must start with uppercase letter)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude test code, generated code, and compliant PascalCase names
never_trigger_on: PascalCase enum names (starting with uppercase, no underscores)
logic.reasoning: Enforces Microsoft's naming convention for enums: PascalCase starting with uppercase letter. Detects enums with lowercase start, underscores, or all-lowercase names. The pattern matches 'enum' followed by identifier that doesn't start with uppercase or contains underscores. Short 2-letter abbreviations like ID, IO are allowed to be fully capitalized per Microsoft guidelines. Scans CompilationUnit source since scanner doesn't extract enum declarations separately.

should_not_trigger: public enum Priority { Low, Medium, High }
